title: lupinus lepidus var. medius
intro: Lupinus lepidus var. medius, also known as the small-flowered lupine, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the lupine family that grows in the mountainous regions of North America. The plant contains isoflavones, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming Lupinus lepidus var. medius is associated with various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of heart disease and improving bone density. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or infusions, although it's essential to note that its consumption can be toxic if not prepared correctly due to the presence of toxic alkaloids.
